stance_summary: The whole normalization (kernel form, e^{iθ} power per radial integral, mass coefficient, measure, prefactor 1/E_cm) is ALREADY PINNED by the locked 2-body test, which uses the IDENTICAL reduced F/(xy) Lagrange rep, the IDENTICAL COULCC functions, and the IDENTICAL back-rotation V. The orthogonal move is to stop reading the paper's symmetric-scaled-Jacobi factors literally and instead DERIVE swift's reduced-rep factors by demanding the 3-body integral degenerate to the 2-body anchor on the y-coordinate. Every factor swift currently borrows from the paper (Hankel bra, e^{6iθ}, m/ħ², 3D C_n) is in a DIFFERENT representation than the one swift's own working code uses.
anchored_assumption: Solvers anchored on WHICH operator combination [V_j+V_k]Ψ̄ maps to (Rxy side / adjoint / Born decomposition). The unexamined anchor I attack instead: that the paper's prefactor (m/ħ², e^{6iθ}, 3D C_n e^{3iθ}, e^{-iqy}/|y| irregular kernel) can be transcribed literally into swift's reduced rep. It cannot — the paper is in symmetric mass-scaled Jacobi with 3D measures; swift is in physical Jacobi with 1D reduced F/(xy) integrals. The conversion is not optional bookkeeping; it IS the missing 4π and the e^{iθ} powers.

candidates.one_line: Derive the 3-body y-coordinate amplitude factors (kernel form, e^{iθ} power, mass coefficient, prefactor) by demanding the swift reduced-rep Eq.17 integral REDUCE to the locked 2-body anchor when the d-n relative motion is treated as the open coordinate — i.e. transcribe the 2-body test_2body_cs_1S0 conventions onto the y-integral verbatim instead of transcribing the paper's 3D factors.
candidates.first_concrete_step: Write a 1-page side-by-side dictionary: 2-body uses bra=regular Riccati F_0(kr e^{iθ})/ϕx (NOT Hankel), exactly ONE e^{iθ} per rotated radial integral, prefactor f=-(2μ/ħ²k²)e^{iθ}M = -(1/E_cm)e^{iθ}M, and B-metric does NOT enter M (it is absorbed in V). Then in test_eq17_green.jl, replace the y-kernel from the irregular ĥ⁻=gc-i·fc to the SAME object the 2-body anchor's bra uses but for the y-coordinate (regular F_λ(qy e^{iθ})/ϕy, exactly as compute_initial_state_vector ALREADY builds ψ_in), keep φ_d(x)/ϕx on the x-side, set the prefactor to -(1/E_cm) with ONE e^{iθ} per OPEN radial integral (the y-integral; the bound x-integral does not contribute a scattering-phase Jacobian because φ_d decays), and drop C_n's extra e^{iθ} power down to match the 2-body (which has NO C_n because the source is already unit-normalized — so C_n in 3-body must use the SAME 1-factor e^{iθ} the bound2b B-normalization already carries, i.e. e^{iθ} not e^{3iθ}). Run the existing 3-mesh smoke loop and report whether |f| jumps from 0.11 toward O(1) and δ moves off 0.
candidates.cost_estimate: 1 day. Pure derivation + edit of build_hankel_bra/run_case in test_eq17_green.jl; existing minute-scale smoke meshes (12x30,16x40,20x50).
candidates.info_gain_if_works: HIGH — pins the kernel + e^{iθ} powers + prefactor non-circularly (matched to a validated anchor, not scanned). If |f| lands near O(1) and δ off 0 with mesh+θ stability, the magnitude bug is the representation mismatch, not the Rxy operator.
candidates.info_gain_if_fails: HIGH — if matching the 2-body conventions verbatim STILL gives |f|≈0.11 δ≈0, then the deficit is NOT in the y-kernel/Jacobian/prefactor (rules out the entire (b)+(c) sub-problem family at once) and forces the residual onto the operator side or the missing channel-spin recoupling (A7), sharply narrowing the search.
candidates.risk: The 2-body anchor has only ONE radial coordinate; the reduction argument assumes the bound x-integral contributes no net scattering Jacobian, which is physically motivated (φ_d decays, no asymptotic flux in x) but is itself a claim that needs the smoke test to confirm via mesh/θ-plateau. If the x-integral DOES carry an e^{iθ} the count is off by one — but that is exactly what the test discriminates.
orthogonality_to_tried: T1 (Hankel bra + m/ħ² + e^{6iθ}, diverged): A5 REPLACES the Hankel bra with the regular F the 2-body anchor proves correct, and replaces e^{6iθ}/(m/ħ²) with the anchor's single-e^{iθ}/(1/E_cm) — opposite factor choices derived from the working test, not the paper.
T2 (ψ_sc-only Hankel, diverged): A5 is kernel/prefactor-derivation, indifferent to ψ_total vs ψ_sc choice; it changes the bra+prefactor, not the wavefunction fed in.

T3 (scale+phase coincidence, rejected as fudge): A5 explicitly does NOT scan — every factor is fixed by equality to the 2-body anchor; there is no free knob to tune, so a hit is a derivation not a coincidence.

T4 (V-protected (Rxy·bra)^T·V·Ψ̄ ordering): A5 keeps WHATEVER ordering is in place and only swaps bra-kernel/Jacobian/prefactor; orthogonal because it operates on the normalization not the operator side (which is forbidden this round anyway).

T5 (bra-type scan hminus/hplus/Freg under T4 ordering, found bra-type irrelevant): A5 differs because it does NOT merely scan bra type — it ALSO changes the e^{iθ} power count and prefactor (1/E_cm vs m/ħ²) and C_n phase to the anchor values simultaneously; T5 held those fixed at the paper's values, which is why swapping bra alone did nothing.
candidates.dependencies: None. Self-contained edit of test_eq17_green.jl; reuses bound2b, compute_initial_state_vector, V_matrix_optimized_scaled.

candidates.one_line: Resolve the mass-coefficient and 4π ambiguity by a UNIT-CHECK derivation: build a synthetic n+d Born-only limit (turn off scattering, ψ_total→ψ_in) where the Eq.17 integral must equal the analytically-known free deuteron-nucleon Born amplitude, and read off the unique (mass factor, 4π, e^{iθ}-power) triple that makes Born come out right in swift's reduced physical-Jacobi rep.
candidates.first_concrete_step: In test_eq17_green.jl add a Born-limit branch: set Ψ̄ = ψ_in (no scattered part), and compute f_Born = -prefactor·C_n^{-1}·⟨bra|[V_j+V_k]|ψ_in⟩. Independently compute the SAME Born amplitude in a transparent 1-line analytic/2-body-style form (the d-n Born term is ∫ φ_d(x)² V_eff(y) F_λ(qy)² dy type object, exactly the structure the 2-body anchor's M_born_unrot already computes for one coordinate). Demand equality and SOLVE for the prefactor: this fixes whether the mass coefficient is m/ħ² (paper, mass-scaled Jacobi) or (4/3)m/ħ²=2μ_y/ħ² (swift physical Jacobi, since swift's y-kinetic coeff is (3/4)ħ²/m so 2μ_y/ħ²=4/3 m/ħ²), and whether a 4π/(2λ+1)/√4π from the bipolar-harmonic Y_α normalization is present in swift's ∫dx dy angular-reduced rep. Report the solved-for prefactor and compare to the three candidate values.
candidates.cost_estimate: 1 day. One added branch in the smoke driver + one analytic cross-check; no new heavy compute.
candidates.info_gain_if_works: HIGH — the Born limit is parameter-free and analytically anchorable, so it pins the (mass, 4π, e^{iθ}) triple uniquely WITHOUT any scattering solve and WITHOUT a scan. Directly answers sub-problem (b).
candidates.info_gain_if_fails: MEDIUM-HIGH — if the Born limit refuses to match any single (mass,4π,phase) triple, that proves the angular reduction itself (bipolar Y_α normalization / the d³x d³y → ∫dx dy step) is where the 4π lives or is mis-applied, redirecting effort to the Gcoefficient.jl normalization rather than the prefactor.
candidates.risk: Constructing the independent analytic Born reference for the 3-body d-n elastic case is the hard part — it must use swift's exact deuteron normalization and the bipolar harmonic convention; if that reference itself carries a convention error the unit-check is circular. Mitigation: anchor the reference to the SAME 2-body M_born_unrot machinery (already validated to δ=63.512°) rather than a textbook formula.
orthogonality_to_tried: T1: A6 turns OFF the scattered wavefunction entirely (Born limit) to isolate the prefactor; T1 used the full Ψ̄ and never separated the parameter-free Born piece as a calibration anchor.
T2: A6 is the ψ_in-only (Born) limit used as a CALIBRATION, not as a convergence test of the scattered amplitude; opposite purpose.
T3: A6 SOLVES for the prefactor from an analytic equality, the antithesis of scanning a free scale; the prefactor comes out as a derived number with units, not a fitted one.
T4: A6 does not touch operator ordering; it fixes the scalar prefactor (mass/4π/phase) that multiplies whatever ordering is used.
T5: A6 is orthogonal to bra-type — it works in the Born limit where the kernel choice and the mass/4π factor are separable, isolating the SCALAR prefactor that T5 never varied.
candidates.dependencies: Cleanest if A5's kernel/e^{iθ} convention is settled first (so the only unknown left in the Born limit is the scalar prefactor), but can run independently with the prefactor and kernel as joint unknowns.

candidates.one_line: Test whether δ≈0 is partly a recoupling artifact: the user takes iel=first(cpl) (a single elastic (λ,J₃) block), but the physical n-d doublet S-matrix requires the FULL Blatt-Biedenharn channel-spin recoupling over BOTH ³S₁ and ³D₁ deuteron channels (scattering.jl recouple_to_channel_spin) before extracting δ — extract the full f-matrix and recouple instead of reading one block.
candidates.first_concrete_step: In test_eq17_green.jl, replace the single-block M=transpose(blk(bra,iel))·blk(W,iel) with a FULL n_deuteron×n_deuteron f-matrix: loop i_out,i_in over ALL coupling channels in cpl (³S₁ and ³D₁ × their λ), build f[i_out,i_in], then call compute_phase_shift_analysis(f, q, α, deuteron_channels, channel_labels) which already runs recouple_to_channel_spin to the channel-spin basis and Blatt-Biedenharn eigenphases. Compare the doublet ²S eigenphase to 105.49° and η to 0.4649. Crucially check: does building the off-diagonal (³S₁↔³D₁ and λ-mixing) blocks move δ off 0 even at fixed prefactor? Run the existing 3-mesh smoke loop.
candidates.cost_estimate: 1 day. Restructure run_case to emit the full f-matrix block + one call into the existing scattering.jl recoupling; minute-scale smoke meshes.
candidates.info_gain_if_works: MEDIUM-HIGH — if the eigenphase comes alive (δ off 0) once the full channel-spin recoupling is applied, then δ≈0 was a single-block extraction artifact, not a lost-scattering-info bug, and the doublet number can be read against benchmark. Answers sub-problem on channel-spin recoupling directly.
candidates.info_gain_if_fails: HIGH — if the FULL recoupled eigenphase is STILL ≈0, that definitively rules OUT the recoupling axis and proves the scattered information genuinely never enters the integral (the Born/ψ_in piece dominates), forcing the diagnosis back onto why ψ_sc's contribution to the matrix element is suppressed — a clean negative that the single-block test could not deliver.
candidates.risk: For a central S-wave MT potential the ³D₁ amplitude is genuinely tiny, so the recoupling may legitimately reduce to nearly the single block and move δ very little — in which case A7's value is the clean NEGATIVE (ruling out recoupling), not a fix. Also requires the deuteron_channels ordering in test_eq17_green to match what compute_phase_shift_analysis expects.
orthogonality_to_tried: T1: A7 changes the EXTRACTION (single block → full recoupled S^J), not the integrand operator/kernel/prefactor that T1 varied.
T2: A7 is independent of the ψ_total/ψ_sc choice; it changes how many (λ,J₃) blocks are assembled before the phase read-out.
T3: A7 introduces no free scale — it routes the matrix elements through the EXISTING validated recoupling (recouple_to_channel_spin), a fixed unitary transform, not a tunable factor.

T4: A7 operates downstream of whatever V/Rxy ordering is chosen; it is about the channel-spin basis transform of the resulting f-matrix, not which side Rxy sits on (so it does not touch the forbidden anchor).

T5: A7 is orthogonal to bra-type — it could be combined with any bra; it asks whether dropping the multi-channel recoupling (collapsing to first(cpl)) is what pins δ at 0, a question T5 never posed since T5 stayed single-block throughout.
candidates.dependencies: Reuses scattering.jl recouple_to_channel_spin and compute_phase_shift_analysis unchanged. Independent of A5/A6 but most informative once A5 has set a sane kernel so the per-block f values are physical.

convergence_note: All three replacement candidates exploit the contrarian lever: the 2-body locked test (test_2body_cs_1S0.jl, δ=63.512°) and swift's own compute_initial_state_vector ALREADY use the reduced F/(xy) rep + COULCC, so the 3-body normalization is DERIVABLE by consistency, not by scanning. A5 transcribes the anchor's kernel+e^{iθ}-count+prefactor onto the y-integral (vs the paper's Hankel/e^{6iθ}/m·ħ⁻²/e^{3iθ}-C_n which are in the WRONG, mass-scaled-Jacobi representation); A6 calibrates the scalar prefactor (mass/4π/phase triple) from a parameter-free Born limit; A7 tests whether δ≈0 is a single-block extraction artifact curable by the existing full channel-spin recoupling. NONE touch Rxy side/adjoint/symmetric-shortcut or Born/ψ-decomposition of Mp (forbidden this round). The decisive discriminator across all three: a HIT must be stable under mesh + θ-plateau with ZERO free knobs, because every factor is fixed by equality to a validated anchor.
